title: Playground Run Monitor
concept: A diagnostics-and-logs console for first-time WordPress learners: the first viewport explains the active run through health, logs, database state, save progress, and risk notices before revealing creation, saved management, Site Manager, Blueprint gallery, import, and export workflows.
focus: database and logs as primary orientation
blue, green, and amber operational states
flows: Monitor current Playground health, logs, database details, path navigation, Homepage, WP Admin, and embedded WordPress preview from the first screen.
Start from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or imported .zip with route-specific inputs and constraints.
Save a temporary Playground in browser storage or to a local directory with separate destination selection, progress, and resulting saved state.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with rename, delete, open, and current-site consequence messaging.
Use Site Manager settings, file browser, Blueprint editor, database tools, and logs while preserving the active site context.
Browse a dense Blueprint catalog with category filters, search, representative cards from 43 entries, selected detail, inspect, and run actions.
Export to GitHub, download a Playground .zip, import a .zip over the current site, download a Blueprint bundle, and download database.sqlite.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, tree, and code editor
inspect Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs
